Phase 3 — Grafana Cloud + Railway OTLP (production observability)
================================================================

Goal: Export logs, metrics, and traces from the Railway backend to Grafana Cloud
so you can query production incidents (OTP failures, /start-interview errors, etc.)
from Grafana and Cursor MCP.

Note: This is OTLP (OpenTelemetry Protocol), not OTP. OTP auth events are included
in logs once observability is enabled.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 1 — Create Grafana Cloud (free tier)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Go to https://grafana.com/auth/sign-up/create-user
2. Create a stack (pick a region close to your users)
3. Note your stack URL, e.g. https://YOURSTACK.grafana.net

----------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 2 — Get OTLP credentials from Grafana Cloud
----------------------------------------------------------------------

In Grafana Cloud portal:

1. Open your stack → **Connections** → **Add new connection**
2. Search **OpenTelemetry** → **Configure**
3. Copy:
   - **OTLP endpoint** (e.g. https://otlp-gateway-prod-us-central-0.grafana.net/otlp)
   - **Instance ID** (numeric, e.g. 123456)
   - **Access token** (create one with `traces:write`, `metrics:write`, `logs:write`)

Keep the token secret — never commit it to git.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 3 — Add variables on Railway
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Railway → soothing-creativity → ai-interview-copilot → Variables → production

Add these (paste real values from Step 2):

  OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED=true
  GRAFANA_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp-gateway-prod-REGION.grafana.net/otlp
  GRAFANA_OTLP_INSTANCE_ID=YOUR_INSTANCE_ID
  GRAFANA_OTLP_TOKEN=glc_YOUR_TOKEN
  OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=ai-interview-copilot-backend
  OTEL_DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT=production
  LOG_LEVEL=INFO

Optional (Railway auto-injects RAILWAY_* — these improve trace correlation):

  OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=service.instance.id=${{RAILWAY_REPLICA_ID}},railway.deployment.id=${{RAILWAY_DEPLOYMENT_ID}}

Do NOT set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to localhost in production.
GRAFANA_OTLP_ENDPOINT takes priority when set.

Click **Deploy** / wait for GitHub auto-deploy after pushing code to main.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 4 — Verify data in Grafana Cloud
----------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Open https://YOURSTACK.grafana.net
2. **Explore → Loki**:
   {service_name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"} |= "auth_"
3. **Explore → Tempo**:
   {resource.service.name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"}
4. Trigger prod traffic: register, OTP verify, start interview

Example OTP / auth log queries:

  {service_name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"} |= "auth_verify_email_failed"
  {service_name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"} |= "auth_resend_otp_rate_limited"
  {service_name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"} |= "otp_delivery"

Example interview error query:

  {service_name="ai-interview-copilot-backend"} |= "start_interview" |~ "(?i)error|failed"

----------------------------------------------------------------------
STEP 5 — Point Cursor MCP at Grafana Cloud (optional)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Option A — Hosted Grafana Cloud MCP (no local Docker):

  Edit ~/.cursor/mcp.json or project .cursor/mcp.json:

  {
    "mcpServers": {
      "grafana": {
        "url": "https://mcp.grafana.com/mcp",
        "headers": {
          "X-Grafana-URL": "https://YOURSTACK.grafana.net"
        }
      }
    }
  }

Option B — Docker mcp-grafana against your cloud stack:

  Run: GRAFANA_URL=https://YOURSTACK.grafana.net ./scripts/setup-grafana-mcp.sh

Then enable grafana in Cursor → Settings → MCP.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
TROUBLESHOOTING
----------------------------------------------------------------------

- No data in Grafana Cloud:
  - Check Railway logs for "Observability enabled"
  - Verify GRAFANA_OTLP_INSTANCE_ID + GRAFANA_OTLP_TOKEN are set
  - Ensure OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED=true

- 401 from OTLP gateway:
  - Regenerate Grafana Cloud access token
  - Confirm Instance ID matches your stack

- Railway redeploy drops last spans:
  - Code calls tracer shutdown on SIGTERM (already implemented)

- Still need Railway platform logs:
  - Railway → service → Deployments → View logs (stdout until OTLP is live)

----------------------------------------------------------------------
LOCAL vs PRODUCTION
----------------------------------------------------------------------

| Env        | OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED | Endpoint                          |
|------------|----------------------|-----------------------------------|
| Local Mac  | true                 | http://localhost:4318 (OrbStack)  |
| Railway    | true                 | GRAFANA_OTLP_ENDPOINT (Cloud)     |

Keep OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED=false locally if you are not running the LGTM stack.
